entry.title: Re: 🆕 SAP CAP Update: CDS 9 Revolutionizes Enterprise Development
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

  • Type as Projection: Reduces redundancy and increases flexibility.
  • Enums as Constants: Improves readability and type safety.
  • Advanced Media Data Handling: Enables seamless image, CSV, and media processing. "

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: New Expert-Guided Implementation: Exploring SAP SuccessFactors HCM Generative AI

entry.title: From reactive to proactive: Use extended situation handling for enterprise projects
entry.content:
Business cases without proactive notification

During each enterprise projects execution phases, manual oversight still been carried out for many activities, such as demand fulfillment, budget overruns, milestones missing, housekeeping after project completion. This led to high operational cost and prone-to error.

Take below 3 cases as example:

• Project procurement controllers manually track impending demand deadlines, a time-consuming process causing missed deadlines and inefficient resource allocation. Teams often waste hours chasing overdue requirements, leading to delays and rework. The lack of proactive alerts forces reactive responses, increasing risks of budget overruns and supply chain disruptions. Controllers require automated notifications when demands near expiration to prioritize actions and prevent operational bottlenecks.
• Project Managers manually track project milestones progress, which is error-prone, causing unnoticed delays that escalate into budget overruns and reactive firefighting. They expect automated real-time alerts to catch missed deadlines early, enforce financial discipline, and protect project margins from cascading risks.
• Project financial controllers face challenges when deviations between sales order revenue and planned project revenue go unnoticed, risking budget misalignment and financial discrepancies. They expect real-time alerts for any changes in sales order revenue, ensuring these values stay in sync and enabling immediate corrective actions to protect project profitability.

Standard situation framework provides predefined situation templates which can only covers a few business cases.

From SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2202, with extended framework for Situation Handling, you can create your own situation use cases with self-modeled situation object/scenario/template. This allows seamless alignment with your enterprise project management activities, helps with proactive risk mitigation through demands delay, milestone missing, budget overruns, revenue deviation and so on, which in the end reduce operational costs and risk.

In this blog, we will walk through a case which leverages this capability for enterprise project demand management: alert Project Demand Fulfillment Delay - If Demand Date is in the past, but Demand Status is not Closed, project manager can be notified. And user can navigate directly to the project demand App for immediate check.

Now Let us create this use case from scratch. As below, multiple roles are involved for this use case.

New apps for the extended framework for Situation Handling

 

App

Roles

Description

Manage Situation Objects

SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC

With this app, you can model business objects that enable Situation Handling. A situation object connects existing application artefacts such as CDS views, events, and actions that form the foundation to set up situation use cases.

Manage Situation Scenarios

SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC

With this app, you can model your own situation scenarios for extended object-based situations. That is, situations that occur for a specific business object. A situation scenario is the technical description of a business scenario and provides the foundation for defining multiple use cases in the form of situation templates, which serve as blueprints for situation types.

Manage Situation Types – Extended

SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT

A situation template serves as a blueprint for a situation type that is used productively and creates situation instances. With the template you define the basic configuration . It is created with reference to existing situation scenario.

My Situations - Extended

Business user

This App shows all situations within your responsibility. It offers context info and actions to solve issues, helping optimize business processes by quickly addressing problems.

Project Demand Notification Sample

Let us look what the notification will be like before getting into the detail steps.
Notification Panel:

By choosing one notification takes you to the My Situations – Extended app. The situation page displays the detailed information as configured in the situation scenario, including the navigation option to a related app as configured in the situation type.

App: Manage Project Demand

Create a Situation Object

App: Manage Situation Objects

With this app, you can model business objects that enable Situation Handling. A situation object connects existing application artefacts such as CDS views, events, and actions that form the foundation to set up situation use cases.
You can use this app to model business objects that enable Situation Handling, including:
• Events that trigger a situation
• Actions that are displayed in the My Situations - Extended app
General Information
Input necessary information and choose appropriate SAP Object Type.

Structures

A structure represents a data source of the business object that can be affected by a situation or that triggers a situation. For object-based situations this is a CDS view. Each CDS view needs semantic keys which uniquely identify an object and are stored in an underlying table. For instance, this could be an ID such as the contract ID, or a combination of fields such as demand ID, demand status, and demand name. In this case, we use standard CDS view I_PROJECTDEMAND.

Navigation Targets
In some cases, there are no specific actions available that could resolve a situation, or a situation may be too complex to be managed with one click. To support the end user, you can define navigation targets to related apps that help solving a situation.
To find the semantic objects, semantic object actions, and parameters of a business app in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library. 
With below config, we define the navigation to Manage Project Demand app which is shown chapter Project Demand Notification Sample.

 

End-User Actions
Finally, you specify the buttons that appear on the situation page in the My Situations – Extended app. Select the callback or navigation target and enter a button name and a button description.

Create a Situation Scenario
App: Manage Situation Scenarios

With this app, you can model your own situation scenarios for extended object-based situations. That is, situations that occur for a specific business object. A situation scenario is the technical description of a business scenario and provides the foundation for defining multiple use cases in the form of situation templates, which serve as blueprints for situation types.

The business object affected by a situation is defined as the anchor object. In the app, you define the anchor object and add all situation objects that serve as trigger object, to trigger situations for the anchor object. For the trigger objects, you define the trigger events and the actions that help the users resolve the situations. And you define which information is displayed in the My Situations - Extended app to the users responsible for solving situations based on this scenario.

The following graphic shows the elements that define a situation scenario:

General Information

Here you select:
• Situation object as anchor object.
• The anchor object structure ID as defined in the Manage Situation Objects app.
The anchor object key field that serves as unique identifier
In addition, you select a responsibility context ID as basis for the situation recipient definition. Contexts are created in Responsibility Management. For more information, see Manage Responsibility Contexts.


Instance Key

Define the anchor object key fields that uniquely identify a situation instance for the anchor object.


List Page Layout

This topic describes how you define the layout (columns, sections, subsections, and fields) for the My Situations - Extended app.
With the Manage Situation Scenarios app, you define for a scenario what you want to display in the list views and on the situation page of the My Situations - Extended app.

For the scenario-specific list page you can define a column for each the anchor object and for the trigger objects:

  • Column label for the anchor object:
    The column label of the anchor object can be specific because there is only one per scenario.
  • Column label for trigger objects:

If you are using several trigger objects in a scenario, choose a generic label, such as Additional Information.
The following graphic illustrates where the two column labels are displayed:

Anchor and Trigger Columns on the Scenario-Specific List Page

Here we define one column for Anchor Object:

General Actions

General actions apply to the anchor object and are available for all anchor-trigger combinations. The input parameters are based on the anchor CDS view. They are selected on scenario level.

Situation Triggers

The situation triggers start the situation engine to check the conditions as defined in the Manage Situation Types - Extended app.

Trigger Objects
A trigger can either be the anchor object itself or another situation object. The trigger object key field needs to be unique and stored in an underlying table.

  • Anchor Object as Trigger
    An anchor object trigger can either be an event, such as the creation, update, or removal of a contract, or batch runs that periodically check the status of the object, such as the expiration date of a contract.
  • Other Objects as Trigger
    Other objects could trigger events. The update of a supplier rating for instance, that results in a significantly worse rating, triggers a situation for the contract with the proposal to select an alternative supplier. Other object triggers are always event-based.

Create a Situation Template

App: Manage Situation Types - Extended

A situation template serves as a blueprint for a situation type that is used productively and creates situation instances. With the template you define the basic configuration. It is created with reference to existing situation scenario.

Situation Instances
The standard settings for the instance behavior are unchanged. Each trigger creates a single situation instance and, after closing a situation, it remains in the system. The situations are displayed in the Instance List of the corresponding situation type in the Manage Situation Types - Extended app.

General Actions
General actions apply to the anchor object and can be both callback or navigation actions. They are displayed for all instances of a situation type on the situation page. For a situation template or situation type, actions that have been defined as general actions are automatically displayed in the corresponding trigger-related action sections (see below). You can add or delete general actions only in the General Actions table.

There is no one-click action for solving such an issue. That’s why a navigation option to a related app is used. The action is configured as a general action because it relates to the anchor object. It is automatically available for any anchor-trigger combination and displayed in the trigger details section as a navigation action.
For more information, please refer Actions | SAP Help Portal. 

Situation Triggers
In this section of the app, displayed for extended object-based situation templates or types, you define how a situation is triggered. An extended object-based situation template or type can have one or multiple situation triggers. The following trigger types are possible:
Event-Based Triggers:
Uses system events to create instances. This trigger type is ideal when you want to inform your users as soon as possible after an issue has occurred. At the same time, you need to make sure that the frequency of events isn't too high to avoid information overload.
Batch-Based Triggers
Uses batch jobs for periodical status checks, especially for frequently changing statuses.
For more information, please refer to Situation Triggers (Object-Based) | SAP Help Portal 

Trigger Details
You use the trigger details to define the conditions that determine if and when users will be notified about situations.
Conditions
In this app section, you define for a situation trigger the conditions for creating situation instances. Each condition needs to have its own texts (situation display) for informing the users about the situation and its own notification behavior, including life cycle transitions.
for example, we can set Demand End Date as ‘=PREVIOUSMONTH’ and Demand Status as ‘!=04100’ which means demand status is not Closed.

Situation Display
In this app section, you can specify the texts with which users are informed about situations. This includes the situation texts displayed in the My Situations - Extended app and notification texts shown on SAP Fiori launchpad. The texts can embed variables that contain information from the anchor object, the trigger object, or from the event.
For the situation display, you can define different texts for the situation description and for the notification, if required by your use case. In addition, you define a text for email notifications. The situation display is assigned to a condition and displayed to the users if the condition is met.
You can also define different situation display texts for each condition, or assign a situation display to multiple conditions.
For more information, please refer to Situation Display | SAP Help Portal

Notifications/Life Cycle
The notification behavior is closely related to the life cycle of a situation instance. The transition from one situation status to another can be a trigger for notifications. For example, the creation of a situation sets the status to Open, and closing it changes the status to Resolved. You can configure a different notification behavior for each condition that is related to a life cycle transition.
You can see the default behavior patterns available for object-based or message-based situations and adjust them for your use case
For more information, please refer to Notifications/Life Cycle | SAP Help Portal

Navigation Actions
The general Display Airline action is automatically displayed in the Trigger Details section.
Batch Job Scheduling
In this app section, you define the scheduling of the batch jobs for batch-based situation triggers. You can choose from a variety of scheduling patterns:

Recipients
With the Recipients functionality, you can identify the appropriate recipients for situations. In Situation Handling, recipients are users responsible for situations of a particular situation type. They are informed about these situations through notifications (if enabled under Conditions) and can view the situation instances as entries in the My Situations or My Situations - Extended app. Please refer here for more information: Recipients | SAP Help Portal.

Create a Situation Type

App: Manage Situation Types - Extended

Situation type defines the detail category of a situation, it is based on situation template as a blueprint. After we us create the situation type with reference to situation template, we can enable it. The configuration of the template can be used right away for a productive situation type. After situation type is triggered by predefined criteria conditions, you can choose Manage Instances to view situation instances for project demand.

Informing End Users

App: My Situations - Extended

After situation type is triggered, the users responsible will get a notification which described in chapter ‘Project Demand Notification Sample’. App My Situations – Extended shows all situations within your responsibility. It offers context info and actions to solve issues, helping optimize business processes by quickly addressing problems.

Further Information

Let me point you out to further information:

  • SAP Help Portal: Situation Handling – Extended Framework for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • SAP Community: Intelligent Situation Handling
  • Custom Situation Cases: Configure Your Own Use Cases (1/6)
  • Help! Why Don’t I Get a Notification for My Custom Situation?
  • Upcoming GenAI features for the extended situation framework:
    1. Generative AI recommendations for Situation Handling
    2. Auto-activation of situation handling use cases for the extended framework
    3. Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

If you want to request a situation to be delivered by SAP in standard, please raise a request via Customer Influence: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for EPPM

entry.title: Re: Yard Logistics: Self Check-In: Empty Error Message wh...
entry.content:
Hi Sandra,

Add below parameter for details message.

Questionnaire used then standard text must be in the operating language. 

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: How to install 2 Diagnostic Agents on a physical host for 2 different Solution Managers
entry.content:
Dear Support,

We have installed a new Solution Manager for the ChaRM configuration but we want to keep the old Solution Manager for the System Monitoring, so we have started to install a second Diagnostic Agent on the Satellite Systems to connect to the New SolMan, but they do not start.
Please, how to make the connection from a Physical Host to 2 different SolMan?

Thank you and best regards

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to install 2 Diagnostic Agents on a physical host...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

I hope this message finds you well.

My name is Haterm from the Diagnostics team, and I’ll be assisting you further with your query.
You are absolutely correct in your understanding. If you wish to add a single managed system to two different Solution Manager (SolMan) landscapes, you can achieve this by installing two Diagnostics Agents (DAAs) on the same host. As outlined in KBA 2385361, each DAA can then be connected to separate SolMan Java systems.
2385361 - How to connect Diagnostics Agent to SAP Solution Manager 7.2 system
 

To ensure a smooth installation, we recommend assigning unique System IDs (SIDs) and instance numbers to each DAA. For example, you could name one "DAA" and the other "DAB," with distinct instance numbers. This will help avoid conflicts and ensure both agents are recognized correctly on the same host.

Should you encounter any issues during the installation or require further clarification, please don’t hesitate to reach out. I’d be happy to assist.

 

Best regards,

Haterm

entry.title: Export from SAP to Spreadsheet > comma separator is changing to dot
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Would like to ask for your help regarding this issue being encountered by a specific user.

He's exporting ALV from SAP to spreadhseet. However, the export is showing "Excel error "We found a problem with some content in '<filename>.xls'" then it will attempt to repair the file. Upon repair, it will remove comma separator and replace it with dot (e.g  from 2,321,858.98 in SAP, export to excel will show 2.321,858.98/ from 282,244.00 SAP to 282.244.00).

Following are the notes and items already checked:

1. Uninstall SAP and Excel - same issue

2. Tried the user to access SAP on my machine - same issue. But if I tried to do it from my end on my machine - no issue (all good)

3. Other users has no issue issue, only this specific has it.

4. Created test ID in test system copied from user's ID in production - no issue (all good). So seems not authorization issue in SAP.

Can you please let me know if there are any additional checks I can perform? Thank you so much!

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One 10.0 FP2305 DIServer always gives "w...
entry.content: The problem got fixed. Old SLD was on port 30000 but the new SLD is on port 40000. Thanks for feedback.

entry.title: Re: Beginner Level Understanding on SAP BTP Architecture
entry.content:
Hello, 

Very informative blog, many thnx. my enterprise is moving away from sap portal, we have all ess/mss apps going to fiori(we do not want to use sap SuccessFactors for now), but there are more than 200 apps namely ( FI, GRC, BW, Knowledge management objects etc) and these apps vary on difference technologies i.e ITS based transaction iviews, WD Java, WD ABAP apps, KM, BW queries etc. Is fiori launchpad good enough to host all of them or is SAP BTP the option? Another question is, can SAP BTP replace our old SAP enterprise portal and host all the services? 
So which one is the way to go Fiori LP or SAP BTP, what did other customers do for similar requirements?

entry.title: Looking for SAP MM Trainee/Entry-Level Opportunities
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’ve recently completed my SAP S/4HANA certification with a specialization in Materials Management (SAP MM) and am now seeking trainee or entry-level roles to kickstart my career.

If you know of any opportunities or have advice, I’d greatly appreciate your guidance. Feel free to connect!

Best regards,

r_chembeyn

entry.title: Want to simulate the transactional data of the Quick model ,i have created .
entry.content: Hi all, want to simulate the transactional data of the Quick model ,i have created and get the insights using SAP Signavio Process Intelligence by uploading a sample transaction data in csv. I am in free trial server of SAP Signavio ,couldn't locate this process optimization in the tool. Please assist for the navigation to figure it out. Uase case for Joule also ..for a prompt .

entry.title: Re: Dynamically filter the values displayed in an Input C...
entry.content:
Yes. It's possible.

If your Input control is based on Booked values, then you need to enable Cascading effect. If you do not any booked Actuals, you can write a data action to initialize valid combinations with a dummy data to enable cascading option.

In case of Unbooked data, this requires a bit of JavaScript to be done.
Example: Let's say you have a Selection_1 for Profit Center Dimension and Selection_2 for Cost Center Dimension.

Cost Center Dimension should have Profit Center maintained as properties.

On selecting Input_Control_1 (Profit Center), use PlanningModel.getMembers function to read all members in the dimension. Loop the result and append to a Dropdown box with filter on CostCenter.ProfitCenter = {InputControlSelection}.

Currently, there are no APIs to append members to an Input control.
You can also use InputControl.getInputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembersWithUnbooked() or InputControl.getInputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembers() but this works more like setting default values based on another Input control selection.

entry.title: Re: Looking for SAP MM Trainee/Entry-Level Opportunities
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/career-corner-job-opportunities/tkb-p/career-cornertkb-board

entry.title: Decentralized manage asset procurement initiation and retirement
entry.content:
Hi Expertise，

I have a requirement to decentralized management to initial asset purchase requisition and asset retirement。 For initiate purchase requisition， it would be wasting asset master number that haven't approved cases; and the raising request to retirement the fixed asset like Equipements and Tools from Production Users also required approval before scrap / write-off and retire from fixed asset module. May I get your option is it available for standard public cloud customer ? If so, which process number for our reference ? Thanks a lot in advance.

BR,
Andes

entry.title: SAP CPI XSLT mapping group by
entry.content:
I want to create XSLT mapping to convert the following source to target xml. Could anyone help? Thank you.

If itemNumber are same, merge transactionalItemData.

Target

entry.title: Re: The Best User Experience Is Consistent – Part 1: About Consistency
entry.content: Great content or information on consistency a usability principle.

entry.title: Re: How to pass multiple values in parameter for data act...
entry.content:
@azm_azm 

The setParameterValue method of DataAction accepts string or array of string as parameter value. So if you want to pass multiple values you can just pass the array of strings rather multiple single values.

setParameterValue(id: string, value: string | string[] | DataActionParameterValue JSON | number): void

 

Just create an array where the members are stored with Hierarchy information. Or try changing the Region parameter of this DA to Hierrachy  "any" and see if it works.

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: BAPI for PA40 Create HR Mini Master Data

  We have Multiple Branch Setup. In Outgoing Payments Branch field is at Header Level & also at Lines Level. What is the difference between the two.

Thanks

entry.title: SAP On Premise EWM System Connection is needed for 2VK Scope Item Implementation
entry.content:
According to the Set up instructions in the SAP EWM Integration - Outbound Processing to Customer (2VK) Scope Item, the information of the SAP EWM On Premise system connection is needed in order to connect it to the SAP S/4Hana Cloud Tenant .

How do we connect SAP S/4Hana Tenant to the SAP EWM On Premise system? What are the necessary connection details?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP On Premise EWM System Connection is needed for 2V...

entry.title: Re: Not Able to do Patch Request for API - API_PURCHASEORDER_2
entry.content: { "error": { "code": "RAP_RUNTIME/025", "message": "Operation is not enabled", "@SAP__common.ExceptionCategory": "Provider_Application_Error", "innererror": { "ErrorDetails": { "@SAP__common.Application": { "ComponentId": "MM-PUR-PO", "ServiceRepository": "SRVD_A2X", "ServiceId": "API_PURCHASEORDER_2", "ServiceVersion": "0001" }, "@SAP__common.TransactionId": "00259DEC28330250E0067E3338D12FB9", "@SAP__common.Timestamp": "20250326050847.657913", "@SAP__common.ErrorResolution": { "Analysis": "Use ADT feed reader \"SAP Gateway Error Log\" or run transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on SAP Gateway hub system and search for entries with the timestamp above for more details", "Note": "See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)" } } } } }

entry.title: Re: Not generating a spool output in the background job

Hi Sunil

entry.title: Depreciation area 01 does not manage values for revaluation
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created an asset master and posted the acquisition, but when trying to post for revaluation this error happens and I am not sure what to do, I have tried searching online and most of the answers imply going into OAYR, AW1NE, OABW, etc. but none of them appear in Fiori as I assume its a GUI TCode. Is there a way to fix this issue or what am I doing wrong?

Appreciate your help in advance,

Misaki

Error Message is as follows:

Depreciation area 01 does not manage values for revaluation

Diagnosis

You are trying to post a revaluation in depreciation area 01.

However, this depreciation area does not manage values for revaluation.

System Response

The posting is not possible.

Procedure

Specify a different business transaction type or a different depreciation area.

 

Message no. FINS_FAA_POST101

entry.title: Re: Trigger event lost in Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Rwitte,

We were able to resolve this issue. In our case Integration Suite Event Mesh was not sending, WebHook-Request-Origin mandatory header. We opened a ticket in SAP and they fixed the issue. 

After the issue is fixed the set up is working correctly if the webhook is configured as mentioned here: Create an Event to Start a Process | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI option is not showing in the Technology drop-...
entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for responding. However, I also tried this with RZ11 with the parameter sapgui/user_scripting value true, and it appears like that. Is there anything else I need to do to ensure that the instance profile is true?

entry.title: Semantic Search in SAP: A New Era of Enterprise Intelligence
entry.content:
Semantic search in SAP applications leverages advanced technologies like natural language processing (NLP), machine learning, and knowledge graphs to interpret the intent behind queries rather than relying solely on keyword matching. This shift enhances data discovery and retrieval in several ways:

  • Contextual Relevance: SAP systems now analyze relationships between data points. For example, searching “revenue decline in Europe” retrieves sales reports, regional performance dashboards, and related customer feedback—even if those documents don’t explicitly include the word “decline.”
  • Unified Data Access: Semantic search bridges silos by understanding connections across SAP modules (e.g., linking a purchase order in SAP S/4HANA to a vendor’s delivery history in SAP Ariba).
  • Multilingual and Synonym Support: Users can query data in their preferred language or terminology. A search for “AP” might return “Accounts Payable” transactions, while “outstanding payments” surfaces open invoices.

By interpreting meaning, SAP reduces time spent on manual searches and helps users discover insights buried in unstructured data, such as emails or PDF attachments.

NLP in SAP Fiori: Conversational Search for the Modern User

SAP Fiori’s redesigned user experience integrates NLP to enable intent-based searching, transforming how employees interact with enterprise systems.

  • Natural Language Queries: Users can type or speak conversational phrases like, “Show me overdue invoices from Supplier X last month,” and SAP Fiori returns precise results by parsing intent, entities (e.g., “Supplier X”), and timeframes.
  • Predictive Assistance: Autocomplete and dynamic suggestions guide users toward refined queries. For instance, typing “sales” might prompt “sales orders created today” or “sales team performance Q3.”
  • Error Tolerance: NLP handles typos, abbreviations, or colloquial terms, ensuring robust results even with imperfect input.

This shift democratizes data access, enabling non-technical users to execute complex searches without training. Finance teams, for example, can quickly locate tax documents using plain language, while HR staff can find employee records by describing scenarios like “employees on leave in December.”

Vector Databases: The Engine Behind Smarter Results

To deliver semantic search at scale, SAP integrates vector databases—a technology that stores data as mathematical embeddings (vectors) to capture semantic meaning.

  • How It Works: When a user submits a query, NLP models convert it into a vector. The database then compares this vector against indexed enterprise data (e.g., invoices, contracts, or transaction histories) to find matches with similar context.
  • SAP HANA Cloud Integration: SAP’s in-memory database now natively supports vector operations, enabling real-time similarity searches. For example, searching for “product delivery delays” might identify logistics documents, customer complaints, and production logs—all linked by contextual proximity.
  • Continuous Learning: Vector databases improve over time by learning from user interactions, ensuring results align with evolving business terminology.

This integration is particularly impactful for unstructured data to offer semantic based investment data. Maintenance teams, for instance, can search “equipment failure last week” to instantly find repair logs, sensor readings, and technician notes—even if those records lack explicit metadata tags.

The Future of Enterprise Search is Semantic

SAP’s semantic search capabilities represent a leap forward in enterprise intelligence. By combining NLP, vector databases, and contextual awareness, SAP empowers users to find answers faster, make data-driven decisions confidently, and focus on strategic tasks instead of manual searches.

As SAP continues to innovate—integrating generative AI for query refinement or expanding multilingual support—the vision of a truly intelligent enterprise becomes reality. Organizations adopting these tools today will not only enhance productivity but also lay the foundation for tomorrow’s AI-driven insights.

entry.title: Re: Problems finding fiori apps in SAP S/4HANA Public Clo...
entry.content:
This is just an working assumption...

Refer to this SAP note: 3495202 - App Maintenance planning Overview not found - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 

That overview page application "Maintenance Planning Overview" had a series of cards in it, that filtered based on Notification & Maintenance Order System Status (It would work if you were using the older scope items BH1, BH2, BJ2).

It looks like if you use Public Cloud with JUST the newer scope items for Reactive & Proactive Maintenance (the old scope items are not activated), then we have moved onto the newer 'Phase Model' and to avoid confusion, it will not show this app in your system.

It is a little confusing, and I'm assuming that its not reflected as depreciated in the Fiori apps library, because it technically is still active (for the older scope).

The recommendation would be to refer to the standard backlog Fiori apps in the Reactive Maintenance scope item instead.

Tim

entry.title: Re: SAP RE-FX intercompany lease ?
entry.content:
Hi in RE-FX you can use contract type "Internal Lease-Out". In this contract type the Contract Category is set to "Internal Contract" and instead of a BP you'll have to assign an "Internal Partner" being a Cost Center, Order or WBS.

This allows you to create the contract in cocd A and use a cost center in cocd B. During the periodic posting the system automatically creates a posting in both cocd A as a revenue and an expense in cocd B.

Regards, Andre

entry.title: Re: Restriction of 999 line items in S/4HANA
entry.content: Hi Sanil, How to resolve this in S4 hana public cloud. Please suggest

entry.title: Re: Readiness Check: Target S/4HANA Version: SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hi ValeskaBoeger,

The answer is in SAP Note 2399707 - Simplification Item Check ;), but to keep it short in SE38 run /SDF/RC_START_CHECK use the download option or if it is not working, you can download the latest Simplification Item Catalog from me.sap.com/sic (click on the release which you need and after on the right middle side there will be a download button and upload it in /SDF/RC_START_CHECK.

Cheers,
Norbert

Although some records are successfully received in the database, but others are failing with this error.
I am using the INSERT action on the DB. What could be the possible reason for this issue?

Detailed error:


Your help would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: How query Execution Happens in Crystal Reports When U...

entry.title: How to deal with Business Partners of deleted users in Solution Manager?
entry.content:
We use the program AI_SDK_USER_BP_GEN to regularly create business partners for all users in Solution Manager. When a person leaves the company the respective user is first locked and after a certain retention period finally deleted. However in this case the business partner in Solution Manager remains existing with the link to the user id in the Employee role and in the identification tab. If the previously deleted user ID is being reused then, there is no new Business Partner created (which is not good) and also the existing BP is not being updated (which is OK, that would create a mess).
 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to deal with Business Partners of deleted users i...
entry.content:
Hi,

From solution manager perspective, we don't support delete/remove BP once the BP is created. But you could try to archive the unused BP by following KBA: ##2640553.

BR.

entry.title: Re: Not Able to do Patch Request for API - API_PURCHASEORDER_2
entry.content:
Which Version of S4 you are working with ?

Please be informed that this version of PO API i.e. V4 Purchase Order API is stable from 2023 S4 Version onwards only. We had a similar issue and had to stick with the V2 version of Purchase Order REST API.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/91af7f8d3acd47da90d33aaacfcd0d59/c89eec80ec2043d980cb7b8c89e0a00a.html

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/purchase-order-v4-post-operation-giving-error-whereas-for-the-same-payload/qaq-p/13594225

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Re: How to Transfer Physical Sample Information from 05 t...
entry.content: Hello @Saquib. Thank you for your response. However, my question was specifically about transferring data from a physical sample created with inspection type 05. In this case, we need to populate the storage location and the type of test tube. This way, users performing a repeat inspection with type 09 can quickly find the sample’s location and identify the test tube it is stored in.

entry.title: Re: How to pass multiple values in parameter for data act...
entry.content: Thank you for your reply Nikhil. I tried changing the Region parameter to "Any" Hierarchy but it seems like I was still getting the same validation error. When I stored the array of strings with Hierarchy information, it worked for me.

entry.title: Problem on archiving PO (Object:MM_EKKO) Error MMPUR_ARCHIVE 091
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I was archiving PO（Object：MM_EKKO）, but there was an error：Delivery costs have not been cleared.

The reason is that there is Delivery Cost in table EKBZ and it is not cleared.

I know the correct approach is to create Delivery Cost Invoice through MIRO or MIR7 to clear the corresponding Delivery cost.

However, our users don't want to do massive recoveries manually.

Also, we check Note#1646578,2026341,2272490 to update the Preprocessing Program,

however, error cannot be eliminated, and PO cannot be archived.

Is there a way to change the error message to a warning message or skip the error message?

Or, any other way to archive PO with delivery cost？

Tks,

Caros

entry.title: Re: How to pass multiple values in parameter for data act...
entry.content: Thank you so much @ihar_stankevich. This script worked for me.

entry.title: Re: Readiness Check: Target S/4HANA Version: SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hi Valeska,

I am sorry, we experienced an issue in development to include the PCE versions within SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA. The functionality is planned to be released within the next two weeks. I will keep you posted.

Thank you for your patience.

Best Regards,
Astrid

entry.title: Manage Components and Services F6178 app availability
entry.content:
Is the Manage Components and Services F6178 app only available on Public Cloud? According to the fiori apps library page it is https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6178')/S34. Same information is also on the Maintenance Backlog Overview F5105 app's fiori library page, F6178 is on the Related Apps tab only if you select the public cloud version.

However, if you select the private cloud/on-premise version of F5105 and go to app documentation, the F6178 is mentioned as a navigation target https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e72f747389b340229f7fa343975bfa57/2bde7398f9074619a9ffb467c13785f4.html

entry.title: Re: First specify the default check rule for the new cred...
entry.content:
Hi,

You may try select a default checking rule in the Configuration step 102623 "Define Checking Rules".

Best Regards,
Fancy

entry.title: Branch field name in VPM2
entry.content:
Hi

  In VPM2 what is the field name of Branch

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting with a sales order stock or a normal s...
entry.content:
The stock (E stock/ normal Stock) should deceived by the filed "Individual and Collective Requirements" in MRP4 View.

Example：

1. For the subcontracting material(F30):  Individual and Collective Requirements = 1

 For the components material of Subcontracting material:   Individual and Collective Requirements = blank

When Run MRP, the subcontracting material and components will be “E” stock, and you can do goods issue for the components to the vendor stock, and when you do the goods receipt for the subcontracting (PO), the stock of components will be consumed.

2. For the subcontracting material(F30):  Individual and Collective Requirements = 2

 For the components material of Subcontracting material:   Individual and Collective Requirements = blank

When Run MRP, the subcontracting material and components will be normal stock, and you can do goods issue for the components to the vendor stock, and when you do the goods receipt for the subcontracting (PO), the stock of components will be consumed.

 

You can choose 1 or 2 regarding to you actual requirement, thank you!

entry.title: Restrict user from submitting Timesheet if all working days do not have working time recorded
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We are trying to restrict end users from submitting a timesheet if all working days in the week do not have a working time recorded. We referred to KBA 2750324 , which works as expected, but it prevents employees from removing a working time entry if they later want to adjust with an absence.

To address this, we created a Save rule on the 'Employee Time Sheet' object (PFA!), but the error triggers even when employees have recorded working time—just not exactly matching the work schedule hours.

Has anyone successfully handled this scenario? Any insights would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your support!

Regards,
Aesha

#SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking

entry.title: Re: how to visualise as end user sales orders created bef...
entry.content:
Hi,

The question was raised based on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Reading db tables is not the way to go.

Best Regards,
Fancy

entry.title: SAP GATEWAY CLIENT IN SEGW
entry.content:
Hello

 

I am trying to create a SAP netweaver gateway service in a demo system. I have successfully created the project, entities & generated objects. I have been able to register the service successfully as well. But when i try to test service for metadata in 'Service Netweaver Gateway Client' , i get below erro due to which i am not able to proceed further. Please help on what steps i need to do to resolve this. Thanks.

 

HTTP Send failed: HTTPIO_ERROR_CUSTOM_MYSAPSSO-

Fehlermeldung beim Senden der Daten.

 

Also, please let me know any generic services that i could use in any system first to check if this connection is correct or not.

Regards,

SABARISH BABU T G

entry.title: Error during export: Failed to open the connection. EmployeeDetails 11832_25760_{0217AE43-E0A5-49B0-
entry.content: Error during export: Failed to open the connection. EmployeeDetails 11832_25760_{0217AE43-E0A5-49B0-8AB9-A65A4585C973}.rptStackTrace: at CrystalDecisions.ReportAppServer.ConvertDotNetToErom.ThrowDotNetException(Exception e) at CrystalDecisions.ReportSource.EromReportSourceBase.ExportToStream(ExportRequestContext reqContext) at CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.FormatEngine.ExportToStream(ExportRequestContext reqContext) at CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument.ExportToStream(ExportOptions options) at CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument.ExportToHttpResponse(ExportOptions options, HttpResponse response, Boolean asAttachment, String attachmentName) at WebApplication1.About.LoadReport() in D:\Alok_Work\WebApplication1 (1)\WebApplication1\WebApplication1\ReportViewer.aspx.cs:line 139

entry.title: Re: SAP GATEWAY CLIENT IN SEGW
entry.content:
Hello,

Check & maintain the following parameters in your SAP system 1st if those are not set already -->

  • login/accept_sso2_ticket = 1
  • login/create_sso2_ticket = 2

Another possible error is at the transaction code STRUSTSSO2. Ensure your certificates are maintained correctly.

Go through the below SAP Notes -->

2425082 - SAP Gateway client (/IWFND/GW_CLIENT) returns error HTTPIO_ERROR_CREATE_MYSAPSSO or HTTPIO_ERROR_CUSTOM_MYSAPSSO

2023484 - HTTPIO_ERROR_CUSTOM_MYSAPSSO - Error while sending by HTTP

Let me know if this helped to solve the issue.

Thanks

Ajitabh

entry.title: Re: Extend a Fiori Application with SAP Web IDE Part 2
entry.content:
hi

Great blog on how to extend the application. we have followed the steps and it is working as expected in webide, but the same when deployed to fiori, it isnt working as expected. Can you suggest on this.

SAPUI5
SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Everyone are talking about MCP... What is MCP and how will it help for your process
entry.content: Additional reference : https://mcp.so/

entry.title: S4 HANA setting for SF Onboarding 2.0 (mini-EC) Integration
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

One of our customers is implementing Onboarding 2.0. They do not have Employee Central and their HRIS system is S/4 HANA system. They were on 1.0 earlier and are now moving to 2.0 due to the upcoming product deprecation announced by SAP.

I understand that integration between Onboarding 2.0 (mini-EC) and S/4 HANA requires a custom integration, but there is some confusion on what configuration settings are required at S/4 side. I know that we need to switch-off Talent Hybrid integration for Employee Data at S/4 first, but need some help with the next steps.

1. Is it possible to have a staging table at S/4 HANA to display onboarding data from SuccessFactors and let HRs manually hire the New Hires to the system (Similar to Onboarding 1.0 integration to S/4)?

or

2. Automated hiring using webservices is the only option and later, HR can key in the tax details and other details manually?

Which among the 2 is the best option? Let me know if there are any other options for integration. Please let me know if there are any good documentation on this topic.

Thanks and Regards

Blessy Akutkar

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding SAP S/4HANA  HCM (Human Capital Management) SAP Cloud for Customer integration with SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: SAP Melbourne Stammtisch April '25
entry.content:
Come and join the local SAP Melbourne Community for a beer and a bite (or whatever you like) on 2nd of April from around 5pm at the Duke of Wellington (2/146 Flinders St).

If you haven't joined us before for a Stammtisch, it is simply a low key social gathering with like minded people from the SAP community. Giving time to allow thoughts to ferment, and experiences to be shared over a tasty beverage is a fantastic catalyst for great conversation. And meeting old and new faces is always rewarding.

This is a short walk from the SAP Business Unleashed Innovation Day being held at the Sofitel:

If your attending the innovation day (and of course if you're not), why not kick on with us to keep the innovative juices flowing?

Please indicate your intention to attend to help me with the booking - but don't worry, you are welcome to come regardless. There are no pre-requisites, just come and join in whatever way suits.

Hope to see you there.

Cheers,
Ben (and the Melbourne Community team @pjcools , @Saikrishna_Sriram),

entry.title: Re: SF Compensation Kick off Template
entry.content: @SG_SF @mkrieg Here (temporary link while they fix the one on Partner Edge which should be tomorrow or so) : https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1rVfP8rZNZ-uNrhvL8uiODif7KKfRwMdL

entry.title: Re: how to visualise as end user sales orders created bef...
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently, most sales order Apps in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition supports filter criteria at creation date level. You may need to implement a custom report based on the standard CDS I_SalesOrder to deal with your time level requirements.

You may also submit an enhancement request asking for filler criteria of sales order Apps to support creation time in https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/1170 using your S-User account.

Best Regards,
Fancy

entry.title: Re: Unable to Read Repository at / Error Connecting to URL Error In Eclipse IDE-SAP BTP / S/4HANA Cl
entry.content: Thanks Vinu, Faced the same issue while installing ADT in eclipse and didn't realize that it was due to certificate. Issue Got resolved now!!

entry.title: Re: Custom Control in Page Variant (Fiori Elements)

Sadly, the methods are not released. Is there a plan to release these methods so UI control developers can use them to store custom state?

entry.title: Re: Handling Compensation Eligibility / Eligibility Excep...
entry.content:
Hi @vinkamath 

Create a Variable (see here for example) with all USERIDs in exception in it. That's how I would build it.

Alternatively in the same link above you will find an example of your idea of adding them to a group in RBP but keep in mind that Comp Administrators will not always have access to RBP admin pages. 

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Compensation – EC Entry Level Field Labels Not Visible
entry.content:
Hi @MariaCarlotaAbreu 

Is the Contract type label showing correctly in EC ? 

I think I've encountered this issue before and the fix was in the EC field setup and not in Comp.

I will double check with colleagues and product team in case they have come across it recently.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SAP FSM - JOIN muliple activites to service call, Err...
entry.content:
There is also a option to call a subQuery, try using that
e.g.
SELECT a.id, sas.name
FROM Activity a, ServiceAssignmentStatus sas,
(SELECT s.object.objectId AS objectId, MAX(s.createDateTime) AS maxcdt FROM ServiceAssignmentStatus s GROUP BY s.object.objectId) sas2
WHERE a.id = sas.object.objectId
AND sas2.objectId = a.id
AND sas.createDateTime = sas2.maxcdt

entry.title: Guidance Needed on Extending Suppliers as Customers in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I need to extend most of our suppliers as customers in our SAP system. Could you please share any tips or best practices on managing this process effectively?

Thank You

entry.title: How to get registered object information from an ABAP program in Developer Extensibility
entry.content:
Hi Exparts,

We are developing custom programs using Developer Extensibility in S/4HANA Public Edition.
We would like to develop programs to perform specific common operations on multiple custom tables.

In such cases, with Classic Extensibility, we could develop flexible functions by dynamically retrieving information from standard tables beginning with "DD".

I cannot find an alternative in Developer Extensibility, so if anyone has any information, please let me know.

Our specific need is to create a program that accesses information that was previously available from tables like:
- DD02L
- DD02T
- DD03L

Please let me know if there are ways to access via CDS views, standard classes, etc.

I would also like to refer to any materials that summarize how to retrieve various registered objects from ABAP programs, if there is one.

Best Regards,
Megumi Inoue

entry.title: Re: SAP GROUP REPORTING LEARNING
entry.content:
Access the learning platform with this link:  SAP Learning.
There is plenty of content on Group Reporting there.
Pretty comprehensive content for configuration in this learning journey: Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Group Reporting (there are also other learning journey mentioned as precondition to this one...)
Happy learning!

entry.title: How to Dynamically Set Values in UI Elements Using a Loop in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Dear All,

  • I would appreciate your help in understanding how to dynamically set values for multiple input fields within a loop.

  • Scenario: I have several input fields that follow an incremental naming pattern.

  • InputField1

  • InputField2

  • InputField3

  • InputField4

  • I want to loop through these fields and assign values to them dynamically.

  •  

Thank you for your assistance!

Details- I want to automate a website to extract the PO Number and input the corresponding details such as Ship Date, Order Total, Agent Name, and more. I need to do this repeatedly within a single loop. While other RPA tools can accomplish this, I am having trouble with SAP BPA. I would appreciate any guidance, best practices, or example implementations for managing dynamic UI elements in SAP BPA.

 

Page link-(https://pathfinder.automationanywhere.com/challenges/automationanywherelabs-supplychainmanagement.html?)

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: Unexpected Journal Entry Appearing in Tax Report with...

Hi,

Check SAP Note 3457501 recording a scenario of the reported case. The same is fixed in SAP Business One Version 10.0 FP2408.

entry.title: Re: Using Ctrl + P on SAP B1 Web Browser
entry.content:
Dear peeraput_nithithanaanan 

As stated in SAP Note 2194215, Some keyboard shortcuts for SAP Business One conflict with the default browser keyboard shortcuts.

Hope it helps.

Thank You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi

entry.title: Re: Object Class List for CDHDR and CDPOS
entry.content:
In T-Code SE11, search for the TCDOB table and enter the table name commonly used in the transaction. By doing so, you can find the Object name.
Example : BOM Table "STPO" >Searching> OBJECTCLAS : "STUE"

By entering the Object name found in this way into the Object field of T-Code SCDO, you can confirm that the table name found earlier is present."
"STUE" >Searching > Can find "STPO" table

"If they match, this Object name can be used as the value for the 'OBJECTCLAS' parameter in the CDPOS table."

"In addition, it can also be used as the value for the 'OBJECTCLASS' parameter of the function module 'CHANGEDOCUMENT_READ_HEADERS'."

"In summary, you can find the types of 'OBJECTCLASS' in the 'TCDOB' table."

entry.title: Re: IDoc with error when trying to update two materials with FM: CSAP_MAT_BOM_MAINTAIN.

entry.title: Re: Is It Standard Behavior for Zero-Quantity Lines to Ap...

Hello @JoseGuill 

Ad 1. Yes, it is.

Ad 2. That is how it is designed.

Ad 3. No, there is not.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAProuter niping error partner not reached
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Would you please help me to solve this problem?

I have successfully run up to “SAP Logon Configuration” as described below.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_EMPLOYEE_CENTRAL_PAYROLL/f4cfcfccbd5940bc9edb7c11fb2e8f2a/bc82d46bf3c4408fba151c3cbe5154aa.html?locale=en-US 

But when I run niping, I get the error shown in the figure below.

*** ERROR => NiBufIProcMsg: hdl 1 received rc=-92 (NIEROUT_CONN_REFUSED) from peer [nibuf.cpp 2028]
*** ERROR => NiBufIConnect: route connect for non-buffered hdl 1 failed (rc=-92;/H/1**.**.**.***/S/3299/H/payroll50.sapsf.eu/S/3299); pong not received [nibuf.cpp 4734]
*** ERROR => NiTClientLoop: NiHandle (rc=-92) [nixxtst.cpp 3417]

*****************************************************************************
*
* LOCATION SAProuter 40.4 on '*****'
* ERROR partner 'payroll50.sapsf.eu:3299' not reached
*
* TIME Wed Mar 26 16:02:00 2025
* RELEASE 754
* COMPONENT NI (network interface)
* VERSION 40
* RC -92
* MODULE D:/depot/bas/754_REL/src/base/ni/nixxi.cpp
* LINE 3454
* DETAIL NiPConnect2: 35.243.***.***:3299
* SYSTEM CALL connect
* ERRNO 10060
* ERRNO TEXT WSAETIMEDOUT: Connection timed out
* COUNTER 15
*
*****************************************************************************

I am also unable to log in to the SAP GUI.

What I have been able to do so far is as follows.
1, SAProuter can be run successfully.
2、The setting of new OSS communication is completed in transaction STC01.
3、SAP GUI setup is completed. (SAP STRING:/H/<my local IP>/S/3299/H/payroll50.sapsf.eu/S/3299)
4, All firewalls are disabled.

Now I am concerned about
1, Is there a problem with the contents of the SAProuttab file?
2, Is the content of the SAP STRING problematic?

And now dev_rout content is below.

Wed Mar 26 15:36:04 2025
NiHLGetHostName: to get ***.**.**.*** succeeded in 4533ms (tl=2000ms)

Wed Mar 26 15:36:25 2025
***LOG Q0I=> NiPConnect2: 35.243.103.152:3299: connect (10060: WSAETIMEDOUT: Connection timed out) [D:/depot/bas/754_REL/src/base/ni/nixxi.cpp 3454]
*** ERROR => NiPConnect2: SiPeekPendConn failed for hdl 17/sock 876
(SI_ECONN_REFUSE/10060; I4; ST; 35.243.103.152:3299) [nixxi.cpp 3454]

Wed Mar 26 16:02:00 2025
***LOG Q0I=> NiPConnect2: **.***.***.***:3299: connect (10060: WSAETIMEDOUT: Connection timed out) [D:/depot/bas/754_REL/src/base/ni/nixxi.cpp 3454]
*** ERROR => NiPConnect2: SiPeekPendConn failed for hdl 18/sock 908
(SI_ECONN_REFUSE/10060; I4; ST; **.***.***.***:3299) [nixxi.cpp 3454]
*** ERROR => NiBufReceive S10/18 'payroll50.sapsf.eu' failed (rc=-10) [nirout.cpp 3369]

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: escape character for apostrophe ' in JDBC receiver channel
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: escape character for apostrophe ' in JDBC receiver channel
entry.content: Hi, I'm getting same error but with (,) in SAP CPI. Can you help me with the resolution?

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hi All,

Iam Lathasree from Andhrpradesh , India.
